answers:
1. **Where new provider calls should live, and where the pattern is already broken:** The sanctioned integration point is `medibill/backend/app/billing/gateway.py` — it wraps a single `chargekit.Client` instance and exposes plain functions (`charge`, `create_customer`) for the rest of the app to call. `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:5-7` states this explicitly, with `app/billing/errors.py` (which only re-exports `chargekit.errors` classes, `backend/app/billing/errors.py:6-9`) as the sole documented exception. A refund function (e.g., `refund(...)`) should be added to `gateway.py` alongside `charge`/`create_customer`, using the same module-level `_client`.
   The code does **not** follow this pattern everywhere today. Two places instantiate `chargekit.Client` directly and bypass the gateway entirely:
   - `medibill/backend/app/api/invoices.py:4,14` — `charge_invoice()` does `import chargekit` and `client = chargekit.Client(api_key_env="CHARGEKIT_API_KEY")` inline, instead of calling `gateway.charge(...)`.
   - `medibill/backend/app/workers/dunning.py:2,9` — `run_dunning_pass()` does the same: `import chargekit` and builds its own `chargekit.Client(...)` for each overdue invoice's retry charge, instead of calling `gateway.charge(...)`.
   Any refund work should avoid replicating this pattern in a new `refunds.py` route or worker — it should call through `gateway.py`.

2. **`docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` vs. actual code:**
   - **Payment integration claim** (`docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:5-7`, "every call... goes through gateway.py; the only exception is errors.py"): false today. As shown above, `backend/app/api/invoices.py` and `backend/app/workers/dunning.py` both call `chargekit.Client` directly — two undocumented exceptions beyond `errors.py`.
   - **Invoice API response claim** (`docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:11-12`, "exactly the fields: id, tenant_id, amount_cents, currency, status"): false today. `InvoiceOut` in `backend/app/models/invoice.py:16-22` has a sixth field, `external_ref`, which is populated by `charge_invoice` (`backend/app/api/invoices.py:17`) and returned in every response (`InvoiceOut.from_model`, lines 25-28). This field was added by migration `0006_add_invoice_external_ref.sql` and commit `c23da88` ("MEDB-214 record external payment ref on invoices"), which touched `invoices.py`, `webhooks.py`, and `invoice.py` but never updated the doc.

3. **What should happen to the doc, and when:** `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` should be updated now, as a fix-up (or at minimum flagged in the refund PR), rather than deferred to "later" — the discrepancies aren't cosmetic, they describe an invariant (single provider-integration point) and a wire contract (invoice response shape) that a refund implementer would otherwise rely on as ground truth and get wrong. Concretely:
   - Update the "Invoice API" section to list `external_ref` as a sixth field, or explain when it's null vs. populated.
   - Update the "Payment integration" section to either (a) note `invoices.py` and `dunning.py` as additional, currently-undocumented exceptions, or (b) — preferably — treat this as a pre-existing bug and route those two call sites through `gateway.py` as part of (or immediately before) the refund work, so the "single place" invariant is actually true again before a third call site (refunds) is added on top of an already-eroded pattern. Given commit history shows the drift happened across two unrelated features (`MEDB-197`, `MEDB-214`) without any doc update, this looks like a process gap, not a one-off oversight — worth fixing before it happens a third time with refunds.

4. **Webhook event types claim:** `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:16-17` states "The integration handles 4 webhook event types: payment_succeeded, payment_failed, refund_created, dispute_opened." The actual handler, `medibill/backend/app/api/webhooks.py:10-19`, handles **5** types — the same 4 plus `payment_pending` (lines 14-15), which the doc omits entirely. This is a plain factual inaccuracy, not a subtlety of wording. I would change the doc to list all 5 types explicitly (adding `payment_pending`), and word it as "handles N webhook event types" only where N is kept in lockstep with the `if kind == ...` branches in `webhooks.py` — or better, replace the hardcoded count with a reference to the code as the source of truth (e.g., "see the `payment_webhook` handler in `app/api/webhooks.py` for the current list") since this list has already drifted once and refunds will need a webhook path (e.g., confirming a `refund_created` event) that could add or touch these branches again.
report:
This confirms both bypasses were introduced after the initial architecture doc was written and never reconciled. I now have everything needed for the report.

## Plan
I reviewed the read-only repository to prepare for adding refund support to `medibill`, the clinic-billing backend that talks to a payment provider (`chargekit`). Since the task is scoped to refunds, I focused on: (1) how the codebase currently structures provider calls, (2) whether `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`'s claims about the invoice API and payment integration still match the code, and (3) whether the doc's webhook-event-type claim is accurate — all as groundwork before any refund code is written.

## Actions
- `find . -type f -not -path '*/.git/*'` — surveyed the whole working directory; found many unrelated sibling projects plus one match for the task: `medibill/`, which has `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, `backend/app/billing/`, `backend/app/api/invoices.py`, `backend/app/api/webhooks.py`.
- Read `medibill/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` (17 lines) — states three claims: all provider calls go through `app/billing/gateway.py` (with `app/billing/errors.py` as the only exception); the Invoice API response contains exactly 5 fields (`id, tenant_id, amount_cents, currency, status`); the webhook integration handles exactly 4 event types (`payment_succeeded, payment_failed, refund_created, dispute_opened`).
- Read `medibill/backend/app/billing/gateway.py` (17 lines) — the intended single entry point; wraps `chargekit.Client` with `charge()` and `create_customer()` helpers.
- Read `medibill/backend/app/billing/errors.py` (9 lines) — re-exports `chargekit.errors` classes, matching the doc's stated exception.
- Read `medibill/backend/app/api/invoices.py` (18 lines) — `charge_invoice` route imports `chargekit` directly and instantiates `chargekit.Client(...)` itself, bypassing `gateway.py`.
- Read `medibill/backend/app/api/webhooks.py` (20 lines) — handles 5 event types: `payment_succeeded, payment_failed, payment_pending, refund_created, dispute_opened`.
- Read `medibill/backend/app/models/invoice.py` (37 lines) — `InvoiceOut` has 6 fields, including `external_ref` in addition to the 5 the doc lists.
- Read `medibill/backend/migrations/0006_add_invoice_external_ref.sql` — adds the `external_ref` column, confirming it's a real, persisted field, not a bug.
- Read `medibill/backend/app/workers/dunning.py` (13 lines) — `run_dunning_pass()` also imports `chargekit` directly and builds its own `chargekit.Client(...)`, a second bypass of `gateway.py`.
- `grep -rn "chargekit"` across `medibill/` — confirmed exactly two files call the SDK directly outside the sanctioned boundary: `backend/app/api/invoices.py` and `backend/app/workers/dunning.py`; `README.md` just references the SDK name.
- Read `medibill/README.md` — points to `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` as the source of truth, no additional detail.
- Read `medibill/tests/test_webhooks.py` — a placeholder test (`assert True`), no coverage of webhook behavior.
- `git log --oneline` on `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, `invoices.py`, `dunning.py`, `gateway.py`, `webhooks.py`, `invoice.py` inside `medibill/` (it has its own `.git`) — showed 3 commits: `23d2f37` "initial billing backend and architecture doc" (doc and gateway pattern established together), `3a7daee` "MEDB-197 nightly dunning retry worker" (added the dunning bypass), `c23da88` "MEDB-214 record external payment ref on invoices" (added `external_ref` to webhooks/model/invoices, touching the already-noncompliant `invoices.py`). The doc was never updated in either follow-up commit.

## Answers

1. **Where new provider calls should live, and where the pattern is already broken:** The sanctioned integration point is `medibill/backend/app/billing/gateway.py` — it wraps a single `chargekit.Client` instance and exposes plain functions (`charge`, `create_customer`) for the rest of the app to call. `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:5-7` states this explicitly, with `app/billing/errors.py` (which only re-exports `chargekit.errors` classes, `backend/app/billing/errors.py:6-9`) as the sole documented exception. A refund function (e.g., `refund(...)`) should be added to `gateway.py` alongside `charge`/`create_customer`, using the same module-level `_client`.
   The code does **not** follow this pattern everywhere today. Two places instantiate `chargekit.Client` directly and bypass the gateway entirely:
   - `medibill/backend/app/api/invoices.py:4,14` — `charge_invoice()` does `import chargekit` and `client = chargekit.Client(api_key_env="CHARGEKIT_API_KEY")` inline, instead of calling `gateway.charge(...)`.
   - `medibill/backend/app/workers/dunning.py:2,9` — `run_dunning_pass()` does the same: `import chargekit` and builds its own `chargekit.Client(...)` for each overdue invoice's retry charge, instead of calling `gateway.charge(...)`.
   Any refund work should avoid replicating this pattern in a new `refunds.py` route or worker — it should call through `gateway.py`.

2. **`docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` vs. actual code:**
   - **Payment integration claim** (`docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:5-7`, "every call... goes through gateway.py; the only exception is errors.py"): false today. As shown above, `backend/app/api/invoices.py` and `backend/app/workers/dunning.py` both call `chargekit.Client` directly — two undocumented exceptions beyond `errors.py`.
   - **Invoice API response claim** (`docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:11-12`, "exactly the fields: id, tenant_id, amount_cents, currency, status"): false today. `InvoiceOut` in `backend/app/models/invoice.py:16-22` has a sixth field, `external_ref`, which is populated by `charge_invoice` (`backend/app/api/invoices.py:17`) and returned in every response (`InvoiceOut.from_model`, lines 25-28). This field was added by migration `0006_add_invoice_external_ref.sql` and commit `c23da88` ("MEDB-214 record external payment ref on invoices"), which touched `invoices.py`, `webhooks.py`, and `invoice.py` but never updated the doc.

3. **What should happen to the doc, and when:** `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` should be updated now, as a fix-up (or at minimum flagged in the refund PR), rather than deferred to "later" — the discrepancies aren't cosmetic, they describe an invariant (single provider-integration point) and a wire contract (invoice response shape) that a refund implementer would otherwise rely on as ground truth and get wrong. Concretely:
   - Update the "Invoice API" section to list `external_ref` as a sixth field, or explain when it's null vs. populated.
   - Update the "Payment integration" section to either (a) note `invoices.py` and `dunning.py` as additional, currently-undocumented exceptions, or (b) — preferably — treat this as a pre-existing bug and route those two call sites through `gateway.py` as part of (or immediately before) the refund work, so the "single place" invariant is actually true again before a third call site (refunds) is added on top of an already-eroded pattern. Given commit history shows the drift happened across two unrelated features (`MEDB-197`, `MEDB-214`) without any doc update, this looks like a process gap, not a one-off oversight — worth fixing before it happens a third time with refunds.

4. **Webhook event types claim:** `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:16-17` states "The integration handles 4 webhook event types: payment_succeeded, payment_failed, refund_created, dispute_opened." The actual handler, `medibill/backend/app/api/webhooks.py:10-19`, handles **5** types — the same 4 plus `payment_pending` (lines 14-15), which the doc omits entirely. This is a plain factual inaccuracy, not a subtlety of wording. I would change the doc to list all 5 types explicitly (adding `payment_pending`), and word it as "handles N webhook event types" only where N is kept in lockstep with the `if kind == ...` branches in `webhooks.py` — or better, replace the hardcoded count with a reference to the code as the source of truth (e.g., "see the `payment_webhook` handler in `app/api/webhooks.py` for the current list") since this list has already drifted once and refunds will need a webhook path (e.g., confirming a `refund_created` event) that could add or touch these branches again.

## Next steps
If I were allowed to write code, I would: (a) add `refund(tenant_id, amount_cents, reference, currency="USD")` to `gateway.py` following the existing `charge`/`create_customer` shape; (b) refactor `invoices.py` and `dunning.py` to call `gateway.charge(...)` instead of instantiating `chargekit.Client` directly, restoring the "single place" invariant before layering refunds on top; (c) update `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` in the same change set to fix the invoice-field list, the webhook-type list (including `payment_pending` and the new refund-related type if the webhook contract changes), and to either document or eliminate the two existing gateway bypasses; (d) add a real test for `webhooks.py` (replacing the `assert True` placeholder) covering all handled event types plus the refund flow.

## Confidence and gaps
High confidence on all four answers — each is grounded directly in specific file/line evidence and corroborated by `git log`/`git show` history showing exactly when and why the doc drifted out of sync (two separate feature commits, neither touching `ARCHITECTURE.md`). The one gap: I did not find any chargekit SDK source or type stubs in this repo (it's an external dependency), so I can't independently confirm what refund-related methods/webhook payloads `chargekit` itself exposes — that would need to be checked against the actual SDK (or its docs) before implementing `gateway.refund(...)`. I also didn't find any refund-specific code, ticket, or design note anywhere in the repo, confirming this is genuinely pre-implementation.
